evals.prompt: Add MIF frontmatter to docs/notes/caching-policy.md — I only know it was created today (2026-06-29) and it's declarative knowledge about our caching policy.
evals.expected_output: L1 frontmatter (id, type: semantic, created) with no fabricated higher-level fields, because only L1 input is available.

deterministic_checks.description: Sets type to semantic (declarative knowledge)
Emits an id field with a non-empty value
Emits a created field
Does not fabricate an L2 namespace field absent input

expectations: The created value reflects 2026-06-29 in ISO-8601 form
No temporal, provenance, citations, or relationships fields appear anywhere in the response

evals.prompt: This doc lives at docs/policies/data-retention.md, is reviewed quarterly, sits under the 'policies' namespace, and was last edited yesterday (2026-06-28). Give me the richest MIF frontmatter the inputs justify.
evals.expected_output: L2 frontmatter adding namespace, modified, and temporal because a review cadence and namespace are known, stopping short of L3.

deterministic_checks.description: Namespace field is set to the stated topic
Modified field reflects the stated edit date
Emits a temporal block for the review cadence
Does not fabricate L3 provenance without attributable sources

expectations: The temporal block reflects a quarterly review cadence, not some other interval
Does not add citations[] or relationships[] since no sources were given

evals.prompt: What MIF level can I claim for docs/drafts/untitled-decision.md if I don't know any review cadence or sources for it yet?
evals.expected_output: Explains the grade-down rule: claim L1 only, do not fabricate L2/L3 fields or placeholders.

deterministic_checks.description: Names L1 as the claimable level
References the grade-down rule by name
Does not suggest writing placeholder values to reach a higher level

expectations: Explains that missing cadence/sources means dropping a level rather than writing a placeholder
Clarifies that type must be one of semantic, episodic, or procedural

evals.prompt: Why is my frontmatter 'type: adr' in docs/decisions/0007-cache-invalidation.md failing MIF validation?
evals.expected_output: Explains that type maps to conceptType (semantic|episodic|procedural); the genre belongs in namespace/tags, and recommends type: semantic.

deterministic_checks.description: Explains the type -> conceptType projection
Recommends type: semantic as the fix for a decision record
Does not claim 'adr' is a valid conceptType value

expectations: Identifies that 'adr' is not one of the allowed conceptType values (semantic, episodic, procedural)
Recommends moving the ADR genre identity into namespace or tags instead of type

evals.prompt: Add MIF frontmatter to docs/incidents/2026-06-15-outage-postmortem.md. It documents a specific outage that happened on 2026-06-15 — a one-time event record, not general knowledge.
evals.expected_output: L1 frontmatter with type: episodic (time-bound record), not semantic, and created reflecting the incident date.

deterministic_checks.description: Sets type to episodic for a time-bound record
Created date reflects the incident date
Does not misclassify the one-time event as declarative knowledge

expectations: Recognizes this as a time-bound record rather than general declarative knowledge
Does not fabricate a namespace or temporal cadence absent any stated review schedule

evals.prompt: I'm documenting docs/research/vendor-comparison.md, sourced from an interview with our vendor on 2026-06-20 and citing their public pricing page (https://vendor.example.com/pricing). Give me the richest frontmatter this justifies.
evals.expected_output: L3 frontmatter with provenance and citations populated from the stated interview and pricing page, layered on top of the L1 floor.

deterministic_checks.description: Emits a provenance field since a real source is attributable
Emits a citations field
Citation references the exact stated pricing URL
No placeholder TODO fields in the L3 additions

expectations: Provenance and citations are populated only with the stated interview and pricing page, not invented sources
The L1 floor fields (id, type, created) are still present alongside the L3 additions

evals.prompt: Add MIF frontmatter to docs/notes/scratch.md. I don't know when it was created — just add whatever frontmatter makes sense.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to fabricate a created date and flags that the L1 floor cannot be met without it, rather than inventing a timestamp.

deterministic_checks.description: Flags the missing created date as blocking rather than proceeding silently
Does not emit a TODO placeholder for created
Does not emit a null placeholder for created

expectations: Treats the missing creation date as blocking the L1 floor rather than inventing a timestamp
Asks the user for the creation date instead of silently guessing one
